summary: tailwind-animate-v4 executes remote payload from blockchain dead-drop on import
details: The package impersonates the legitimate tailwindcss-animate plugin (same author string, near-identical plugin body) but appends a hidden loader at the end of index.js. After whitespace padding, an eval(atob(...)) block decodes a loader that fetches transaction data from api.trongrid.io, fullnode.mainnet.aptoslabs.com, bsc-dataseed.binance.org, and bsc-rpc.publicnode.com, extracts hex-encoded payload bytes from on-chain transaction inputs, XOR-decodes them with hardcoded keys, and eval()s the result. It then spawns a detached, hidden `node -e` child process (detached:true, stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true) to persist a second-stage payload beyond the initial require. This fires on any require()/import of the package, giving whoever controls the blockchain dead-drop arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine. The name is a typosquat of tailwindcss-animate, and the on-chain C2 pattern is designed to evade takedown of static hosts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
